content:
📜 3. Work, Healthcare, and Interdisciplinary Applications

Every week, students examine literature through three lenses:
1. Work & Professions → Healthcare, Law, AI, Journalism, Ethics
2. Family & Social Life → How literature shapes relationships, home life, psychology
3. Macro-Level Cultural & Philosophical Impact → The broadest lens, tying literature into larger questions about humanity

Example:
📖 Week 4 – The Gothic Mind (Fear, Perception, & Madness)
• Work Lens: How mental illness was misdiagnosed in the 19th century & literary depictions of psychological horror
• Family Lens: How repression & isolation shaped Gothic literature
• Macro Lens: The role of paranoia, conspiracy theories, and social fear in shaping art

✅ Why This Works:
• Ensures relevance to multiple disciplines.
• Encourages students to engage personally & socially with literature.

📜 4. Historical Anchor Section

Each week will connect literature to a timeline of historical echoes:
